perturbation.original_text: 1.3 "Affiliate" means, with respect to any Person, any Person which, directly or indirectly, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with, the specified Person. For purposes of this definition, the term "control" as applied to any Person, means the possession, directly or indirectly, of at least fifty-one per cent (51%) of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management of that Person, whether&sbsp;through ownership of voting securities or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 "Affiliate" means, with respect to any Person, any Person which, directly or indirectly, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with, the specified Person. For purposes of this definition, the term "control" as applied to any Person, means the possession, directly or indirectly, of at least twenty-five per cent (25%) of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management of that Person, whether&sbsp;through ownership of voting securities or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Affiliate' is changed to define control as 25% ownership instead of 51%. This creates an in-text contradiction because other sections of the contract may rely on the original 51% definition to determine the scope of liabilities, rights or obligations between affiliates, thus creating conflict of interest or in contract obligations
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon, and shall inure to the benefit of successors of the Parties hereto, or to any assignee of all of the goodwill and entire business assets of a Party hereto relating to pharmaceuticals, but shall not otherwise be assignable without the prior written consent of the other Party. For the avoidance of doubt, LEO agrees and acknowledges that GALEN may perform any or all of its obligations under this agreement through its U.S. Affiliates, Warner Chilcott, Inc..
perturbation.justification: The modification to the definition of 'Affiliate' from 51% control to 25% creates a direct contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text'. The original text allows GALEN to perform obligations through its U.S. Affiliates, specifically Warner Chilcott, Inc. The changed definition now includes entities with only 25% control as affiliates. This potentially broadens the scope of entities that can fulfill GALEN's obligations, which may not have been the intention when the agreement was drafted with the 51% threshold. This represents a strong in-text contradiction because it alters the entities covered under the agreement, directly impacting obligations, creating ambiguity and creating conflicting obligations in the contract based on the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 All Technical Information disclosed to GALEN and all GALEN Information disclosed to LEO shall be considered confidential regardless of designation, and shall not be disclosed by the receiving Party to any third party or used outside the scope of this Agreement without the prior written consent of the disclosing Party except to a duly authorised Governmental or Regulatory Authority in connection with the registration or regulation of the Combination Product or if otherwise required by Law. In the event that a Party is asked to disclose any confidential information to a Governmental or Regulatory Authority, such Party will - if possible - notify the nondisclosing Party sufficiently prior to making such disclosure so as to allow the nondisclosing Party adequate time to take whatever action it may deem to be appropriate to protect the confidentiality of the information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 All Technical Information disclosed to GALEN and all GALEN Information disclosed to LEO shall be considered confidential regardless of designation, and may be disclosed by the receiving Party to any third party or used outside the scope of this Agreement without the prior written consent of the disclosing Party, especially in connection with the registration or regulation of the Combination Product or if otherwise required by Law. In the event that a Party is asked to disclose any confidential information to a Governmental or Regulatory Authority, such Party will - if possible - notify the nondisclosing Party sufficiently prior to making such disclosure so as to allow the nondisclosing Party adequate time to take whatever action it may deem to be appropriate to protect the confidentiality of the information.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text in section 5.1 now states that Technical and GALEN information 'may be disclosed' instead of 'shall not be disclosed,' contradicting the confidential nature described earlier in the same sentence. The contradiction arises because the section begins establishing confidentiality but then introduces an exception that essentially nullifies that confidentiality, particularly when required by law or for regulation. This in-text contradiction makes the obligation of confidentiality unenforceable, especially that the initial section stated it as 'regardless of designation'
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Technical Information disclosed to GALEN and all GALEN Information disclosed to LEO shall be considered confidential regardless of designation, and may be disclosed by the receiving Party to any third party or used outside the scope of this Agreement without the prior written consent of the disclosing Party, especially in connection with the registration or regulation of the Combination Product or if otherwise required by Law. In the event that a Party is asked to disclose any confidential information to a Governmental or Regulatory Authority, such Party will - if possible - notify the nondisclosing Party sufficiently prior to making such disclosure so as to allow the nondisclosing Party adequate time to take whatever action it may deem to be appropriate to protect the confidentiality of the information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Technical Information 'shall not be disclosed', establishing a strict confidentiality obligation. The changed text modifies this to 'may be disclosed', directly contradicting the initial confidentiality obligation. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the two clauses cannot coexist: information cannot simultaneously be subject to a strict non-disclosure obligation and also be permissibly disclosed. The phrase 'regardless of designation' further emphasises the original intent of strict confidentiality, which is undermined by the 'may be disclosed' language. This contradiction introduces ambiguity regarding the enforceability of the confidentiality provision, potentially leading to legal disputes.

perturbation.original_text: XI - FORCE MAJEURE The occurrence of an event which materially interferes with the ability of a Party to perform its obligations or duties hereunder which is not within the reasonable control of the Party affected, not due to malfeasance, and which could not with the exercise of due diligence have been avoided ("Force Majeure") including, but not limited to, fire, accident, labour difficulty, strike, riot, civil commotion, act of God, delay or errors by shipping companies or change in Law shall not excuse such Party from the performance of its obligations or duties under this Agreement, but shall merely suspend such performance during the continuation of Force Majeure.
perturbation.changed_text: XI - FORCE MAJEURE The occurrence of an event which materially interferes with the ability of a Party to perform its obligations or duties hereunder which is not within the reasonable control of the Party affected, not due to malfeasance, and which could not with the exercise of due diligence have been avoided ("Force Majeure") including, but not limited to, fire, accident, labour difficulty, strike, riot, civil commotion, act of God, delay or errors by shipping companies or change in Law shall excuse such Party from the performance of its obligations or duties under this Agreement and suspend such performance during the continuation of Force Majeure.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that Force Majeure 'shall not excuse' performance, but merely suspends it. The changed text states that Force Majeure 'shall excuse' performance. This creates a direct contradiction in the same section. It creates an in-text contradiction because now the party won't be liable to continue performing the duties as under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The occurrence of an event which materially interferes with the ability of a Party to perform its obligations or duties hereunder which is not within the reasonable control of the Party affected, not due to malfeasance, and which could not with the exercise of due diligence have been avoided ("Force Majeure") including, but not limited to, fire, accident, labour difficulty, strike, riot, civil commotion, act of God, delay or errors by shipping companies or change in Law shall excuse such Party from the performance of its obligations or duties under this Agreement and suspend such performance during the continuation of Force Majeure. The Party prevented from performing its obligations or duties because of Force Majeure shall promptly notify the other Party hereto (the "Other Party") of the occurrence and particulars of such Force Majeure and shall provide the Other Party, from time to time, with its best estimate of the duration of such Force Majeure and with notice of the termination thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that a Force Majeure event 'shall not excuse' a party's performance, while the changed text states that it 'shall excuse' such performance. This creates a direct, impactful, and irreconcilable contradiction within the same section of the document, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Ambiguities' perturbation type, making performance obligations unclear and potentially unenforceable.
